name: snmalloc
summary: Microsoft Research lock-free allocator that replaces thread-local cross-thread free caches with a Pony-derived MPSC "return-to-owner" message queue, flushing thousands of remote frees with a single atomic exchange.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Microsoft Research's snmalloc is built around the observation that producer-consumer workloads punish standard thread-cache designs — the consumer thread accumulates orphaned blocks that the producer thread originally allocated, so freeing them requires expensive cross-thread synchronisation. snmalloc instead batches deallocations from foreign threads onto a lock-free MPSC queue and "returns them to owner" with a single atomic exchange — no CAS loop.

It is the default malloc in the Verona language runtime and the cleanest answer in the literature to producer-consumer cross-thread free patterns.

sections.title: Return-to-owner message passing
sections.content:
The core mechanism is borrowed from Pony's actor runtime: each thread owns its allocations and is the only thread that can put a freed block back onto its allocator's free list. When thread B frees a block originally allocated by thread A, B pushes the block onto an MPSC queue attached to A's allocator. Thread A drains the queue lazily on its own allocation slow path with a single `XCHG`. There is no per-block atomic on the cross-thread free path.

A hybrid bump-pointer plus free-list scheme stores all per-slab metadata in 64 bits per 64 KiB slab. Recent versions hold metadata out-of-band behind guard pages, encode in-band metadata to detect corruption, and use a "combining lock" (MCS plus flat combining) to cut startup contention.

sections.title: Deployment
sections.content: snmalloc is the default malloc in Microsoft Research's Verona language runtime. It is also available as a Rust crate and as an `LD_PRELOAD` shim for general C/C++ programs. The design biases toward producer-consumer workloads (many message-passing systems, queues, and actor runtimes) where its return-to-owner advantage compounds.
